issues.summary: API response building uses JSONObject in some handlers and Map<String,Object> in others within the same class
evidence: handleFullSketch, handleRelationQuery, handleFullDependencySketch, handleDependencyRelationQuery all build responses with JSONObject directly
handleSemanticFieldExplore, handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti, handleSemanticField, handleConcordanceExamples all build responses with Map<String, Object>
Both styles are passed to the same HttpApiUtils.sendJsonResponse(exchange, response)
issues.suggestion: Adopt a single style throughout WordSketchApiServer. The JSONObject approach avoids an extra serialization step; alternatively introduce typed response records and convert to JSON in one place.

issues.summary: handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti passes the raw pattern without headword substitution, unlike the parallel handleSemanticFieldExplore
evidence: handleSemanticFieldExplore: String bcqlPattern = relationConfig.get().getFullPattern(seed);  // substitutes headword
handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti: String bcqlPattern = relationConfig.get().pattern();  // raw pattern, no substitution
Both methods then pass bcqlPattern to executor.executeSurfacePattern
issues.suggestion: Call getFullPattern(seed) instead of pattern() for each seed inside the loop in handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti, matching the single-seed handler.

issues.summary: BlackLabQueryExecutor exposes four partially-overlapping collocate-finding methods with no clear boundary
evidence: findCollocations – CQL-based, returns WordSketchResult
findDependencyCollocations – dependency BCQL, returns WordSketchResult
executeDependencyPattern – dependency BCQL with POS constraint, public, returns WordSketchResult
executeSurfacePattern – labeled BCQL, public, returns WordSketchResult
All four share buildAndRankCollocates at the end but approach the problem differently
issues.suggestion: Consolidate findCollocations and findDependencyCollocations into executeSurfacePattern/executeDependencyPattern, retiring the old public API; document the intended usage difference between the two remaining methods.

issues.summary: getCorpusSize() stub in BlackLabQueryExecutor is misindented and silently returns 0 without any indication it is unimplemented
evidence: public long getCorpusSize() throws IOException { return 0L; }  // body at column 8, not 4
No @Override, no TODO, no UnsupportedOperationException — the stub is invisible to callers
issues.suggestion: Either implement the method properly or throw UnsupportedOperationException and annotate with // TODO, and fix the indentation.

issues.summary: QueryExecutor.executeSurfacePattern default silently discards headPosition and collocatePosition parameters
evidence: default List<QueryResults.WordSketchResult> executeSurfacePattern(String lemma, String bcqlPattern, int headPosition, int collocatePosition, double minLogDice, int maxResults) throws IOException { return findCollocations(lemma, bcqlPattern, minLogDice, maxResults); }
The Javadoc for headPosition and collocatePosition does not mention that they are ignored in the default implementation
issues.suggestion: Add a Javadoc note on the default implementation stating that headPosition and collocatePosition are ignored; or throw UnsupportedOperationException to force overriding.

evidence: static String extractCollocateFromPattern(String bcqlPattern, int hitIndex) { int pos2Index = findLabelPosition(bcqlPattern, 2); if (pos2Index <= 0) { return "unknown"; } return "unknown"; }
Both branches return "unknown"; the method body is dead code
issues.suggestion: Remove the method or implement it; any caller receiving 'unknown' silently gets a wrong collocate label.

issues.summary: Numeric query parameters are parsed with Integer.parseInt/Double.parseDouble without catching NumberFormatException, producing opaque 500 errors for malformed input
evidence: int topCollocates = Integer.parseInt(params.getOrDefault("top", "15"));
double minLogDice = Double.parseDouble(params.getOrDefault("min_logdice", "3.0"));
Same pattern repeated in handleSemanticFieldExplore, handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti, handleSemanticField, handleConcordanceExamples
issues.suggestion: Wrap numeric parsing in try/catch and return HTTP 400 with a descriptive message on NumberFormatException.

issues.summary: BlackLabSnippetParser.extractPosFromMatch returns the string 'unknown' instead of null when no POS is found, leaking a sentinel into data
evidence: static String extractPosFromMatch(String matchText) { ... return "unknown"; }
The returned sentinel propagates through WordSketchResult.pos and appears in API JSON responses as a real POS value
issues.suggestion: Return null when no POS is found; callers that need a display string can substitute 'unknown' at the display layer.

issues.summary: convertConlluToWplChunks compresses four state-machine updates onto one line, obscuring the logic
evidence: w.close(); w = null; chunks++; sentencesInChunk = 0;  // four operations on one line
The null-writer state (w == null means 'no current chunk file') is an implicit sentinel not explained
issues.suggestion: Expand to four separate statements and add a brief comment explaining the null sentinel: // null means no open chunk file

issues.summary: GrammarConfigLoader re-implements the same [bracket]-token scanning loop three times
evidence: countPatternTokens(String pattern) – scans for [token] blocks counting them
deriveTokenPosition(String pattern, char targetLabel, int defaultPos) – scans [token] blocks counting and matching a label
substituteHeadword(String pattern, String headword, int headPosition) – scans [token] blocks building a positions list
All three use identical while-loop skeletons but cannot be shared because they return different things
issues.suggestion: Extract a shared tokenizePattern(String) helper that returns a List<String> of bracket-tokens; reuse it in all three places.

issues.summary: handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti uses null as a sentinel for 'no intersection computed yet', which is non-obvious
evidence: Set<String> commonCollocates = null;
if (commonCollocates == null) { commonCollocates = seedCollocates; } else { commonCollocates.retainAll(seedCollocates); }
if (commonCollocates == null) { commonCollocates = new HashSet<>(); }  // after loop
issues.suggestion: Replace the null sentinel with a boolean flag or an Optional, or initialize with the first seed's collocates before the loop; add a comment explaining the intent.

issues.summary: RelationConfig.relationType is a raw String that only takes two values, used in 8+ string equality checks
evidence: String relationType inside RelationConfig record
"SURFACE".equals(rel.relationType()) and "DEP".equals(rel.relationType()) repeated across handleFullSketch, handleRelationQuery, handleFullDependencySketch, API listing endpoints
issues.suggestion: Replace relationType with an enum RelationType { SURFACE, DEP } and use switch/pattern matching; the compiler will enforce exhaustiveness.

issues.summary: PosGroup constants are raw Strings; comparisons like collocatePosGroup().equals(PosGroup.ADJ) cannot be verified by the compiler
evidence: public static final String ADJ = "adj"; in PosGroup
collocatePosGroup().equals(PosGroup.ADJ) in handleSemanticFieldExplore and handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti
posGroupFromConstraint returns "prep", "part", "other" which are not even in PosGroup
issues.suggestion: Turn PosGroup into an enum (NOUN, VERB, ADJ, ADV, PREP, PART, OTHER); update collocatePosGroup() return type accordingly.

issues.summary: API response building throughout WordSketchApiServer uses Map<String, Object>, making property name typos undetectable
evidence: Map<String, Object> response = new HashMap<>(); response.put("status", "ok"); response.put("seeds", ...) ...
Same pattern in handleSemanticFieldExplore, handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti, handleSemanticField, handleConcordanceExamples, handleBcqlQueryPost
issues.suggestion: Introduce typed response records (Java records or inner static classes) and serialize via fastjson2; property names become field names visible to the compiler.

issues.summary: convertConlluToWplChunks returns long[] to encode three semantically distinct counts with no positional safety
evidence: long[] counts = convertConlluToWplChunks(Paths.get(inputPath), wplTempDir, 10_000);
printf uses counts[0], counts[1], counts[2] in positional order: sentences, tokens, chunks
issues.suggestion: Return a record WplConversionStats(long sentences, long tokens, long chunks) or use a three-element named structure.

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer uses 'adjective' terminology pervasively for what is a general-purpose collocate concept
evidence: Inner class CoreAdjective – used for any collocate, not just adjectives
Inner class AdjectiveProfile – used in comparison of any relation
ExplorationResult.seedAdjectives: Map<String, Double> – holds collocates of any POS
SemanticFieldExplorer.compare() builds 'adjectiveProfiles' for all noun collocates
logger.info("Core {} (define the class):", relationName) attempts to abstract the concept but the field names don't
issues.suggestion: Rename CoreAdjective → CoreCollocate, AdjectiveProfile → CollocateProfile, seedAdjectives → seedCollocates, coreAdjectives → coreCollocates, nounProfiles → candidateProfiles.

issues.summary: BlackLabSnippetParser.extractCollocateFromPattern is named as if it extracts data but always returns 'unknown'
evidence: static String extractCollocateFromPattern(String bcqlPattern, int hitIndex) { ... return "unknown"; }
issues.suggestion: Remove or fully implement the method; if left as a stub, rename it to something like getDefaultCollocate() and annotate // TODO: implement.

issues.summary: BufferedWriter named 'w' in convertConlluToWplChunks is a single-letter variable in a non-trivial 50-line method
evidence: BufferedWriter w = null; ... w.write("<s>\n"); ... w.close(); w = null;
issues.suggestion: Rename to 'chunkWriter' or 'currentChunk' to signal intent.
